title: Tandem Crane Lift Plan Review - Roof Truss T-1 and T-2
description: Reviewed engineered lift plan for tandem pick of primary roof trusses T-1 and T-2 using Liebherr 1750 and Manitowoc 18000 working in tandem. Covered load sharing calculations communication protocol between operators signal person responsibilities exclusion zone dimensions and abort criteria including wind speed limits of 18 mph maximum. Load chart review for both cranes at working radii.
notes: All ironworkers riggers crane operators and signal persons attended. Lift plan signed by all participants. Pre-lift meeting conducted morning of each pick. Lift director Mark Schultz NCCO-certified conducted meeting.

title: Fall Protection at Heights Above 6 Feet - Stadium Bowl and Elevated Work
description: Reviewed OSHA 1926 Subpart M requirements applicable to stadium bowl elevated work. Demonstrated proper harness fitting and D-ring positioning for both climbing and work positioning. Covered 100% tie-off policy at or above 6 feet. Anchor point selection for concrete and steel work. Covered leading edge work requirements for deck and PT tendon crews. Reviewed fall clearance calculations for self-retracting lifelines.
notes: All elevated trades attended including ironworkers concrete crews PT tendon crew and elevated MEP workers. 4 harnesses removed from service as failed inspection. Distributed updated harness inspection checklist cards.

title: Struck-by Hazard Awareness - Crane Picks and Overhead Work
description: Addressed struck-by hazards during major crane activity on site. Covered exclusion zones during picks and who has authority to enter. Spotters role and responsibility. Flying debris from grinding cutting and impact tools. Vehicle backing and pedestrian interaction. Covered 100% hard hat rule and correct suspension and adjustment for impact absorption.
notes: All trades on site attended in two sessions. Translated to Spanish for 68 Spanish-speaking workers. Distributed exclusion zone map showing all active crane radii for the week.

title: Concrete Pump Safety - High Pressure Systems
description: Reviewed hazards associated with high-pressure concrete pumping operations. Covered inspection requirements for pump hoses and couplers including visual inspection before each pour and replacement intervals. Emergency shutdown procedures and location of all pump shutoffs. Cleaned and capped hose storage requirements. Discussed February 19 hose failure incident and lessons learned. No worker fatalities in industry are more preventable than concrete pump hose failures.
notes: All concrete crews pump operators and adjacent trades attended. New hose inspection log distributed and posted at each pump. Reinforced that any worker observing hose deterioration has authority to stop the pour.

title: Heat Illness Prevention - Miami Summer Work Planning
description: Reviewed OSHA heat illness prevention requirements specific to South Florida outdoor construction. Covered heat index chart and work-rest ratios at each alert level. Location of all water stations shaded rest areas and cooling stations on site. Signs and symptoms of heat exhaustion and heat stroke. Emergency action plan for heat illness including who to call and where to send worker. Covered acclimatization schedule for new workers.
notes: All site personnel attended across three 8am sessions. Water station locations marked on updated site map distributed to all foremen. Heat illness emergency response cards distributed to all supervisors. Spanish language materials provided.

title: Elevated Concrete Pour Safety - Post-Tension Slab Procedures
description: Reviewed safety requirements specific to post-tension slab work. Covered tendon stressing operations exclusion zones and required PPE including face shields. Discussed PT anchor zone blow-out hazard and inspection requirements before stressing. Communication protocol between PT crew and general work crews during stressing operations. Reviewed HOK structural notes for prohibited re-entry periods after stressing.
notes: All PT tendon crew concrete crew and supervisors for floors with PT slab pours attended. PT crew from VSL International attended and presented. Corrective action from prior project near miss discussed.
